name: e07 - Currency Conversion and Graceful Degradation

nodes.name: Apply manual rate; record date + source = manual
nodes.documentation: baseAmount = round(amount * PO.exchangeRate, 2). Persist the FX context on the line/PO: rate value, rate date (today or the buyer-entered date), source = 'manual', enteredBy. No external call. [SCOR OE4 | source: AB override + review FX-context]

nodes.name: Fetch live rate (Frankfurter)
nodes.documentation: Call the FX provider (e.g. Frankfurter) for currency -> baseCurrency. The call is wrapped so it NEVER throws to the caller; any transport/HTTP error is captured and turned into a fetch-failure flag. [SCOR OE4 | source: platform-services FX + RA e08 graceful degradation]

nodes.name: baseAmount = round(amount * rate, 2)
nodes.documentation: Convert: baseAmount = round(amount * rate, 2) (2 decimal places). Persist the FX context: rate value, rate date = fetch date, source = provider name (e.g. 'Frankfurter'). The base amount now drives approval thresholds, landed-cost comparison, and analytics. [SCOR OE4 | source: platform-services FX convert + review FX-context]

nodes.name: Log + return original amount unconverted
nodes.documentation: Graceful degradation: log the reason (fetch-failure | invalid-rate) and return the ORIGINAL amount unchanged; mark the FX context source = 'unconverted', rate = null so downstream knows the figure is not normalized. convertToBase NEVER throws; the cycle continues rather than blocking on an FX outage. A flagged unconverted amount can be re-converted later. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA e08 never-throws graceful degradation]

nodes.name: Persist base amount + FX context (rate, date, source)
nodes.documentation: Store totalAmountInBase (or line baseAmount), exchangeRate, fxRateDate, fxSource on the entity. The FX context is required so the realized FX gain/loss can later be computed PO-date-vs-payment-date. Emits SSE; the figure is now comparable across currencies. [SCOR OE4 | source: review FX gain/loss addition]

nodes.name: Compute realized FX gain/loss at payment
nodes.documentation: At payment time, Finance computes realized FX gain/loss = (PO-date base value) - (payment-date base value) for the same foreign amount, using each date's rate from the stored FX context. Posts the gain/loss to the ledger and surfaces it on the creditor ledger and analytics. This step only runs for foreign-currency settlements. [SCOR S2.7 | source: review realized FX gain/loss PO-vs-payment]

nodes.name: Per-PO manual exchange rate set?
nodes.documentation: Expression: PO.exchangeRate != null (buyer set a manual override on this PO). True -> use the manual rate directly (baseAmount = round(amount * PO.exchangeRate, 2)), skip the live fetch; the override carries its own rate date + source = 'manual'. False -> call the FX service. The override exists for contracts that fix a rate or where the buyer must match a bank rate (AB per-PO override). [SCOR OE4 | source: AB per-PO manual exchange-rate override]

nodes.name: currency is base or null?
nodes.documentation: Expression: currency == baseCurrency OR currency == null. True -> return the amount UNCHANGED and make NO external call (no conversion needed; rate implicitly 1.0). False (a foreign currency) -> fetch a live rate. This guard prevents needless calls and is the first branch of convertToBase. [SCOR OE4 | source: platform-services FX convertToBase base/null short-circuit]

nodes.name: Fetch succeeded?
nodes.documentation: Expression: the provider returned a 2xx response with a parseable rate. False (timeout, non-2xx, network error, unparseable body) -> LOG the failure and RETURN THE ORIGINAL amount unconverted (graceful degradation). True -> validate the rate value. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA e08 fetch-failure -> return original]

nodes.name: Rate finite and > 0?
nodes.documentation: Expression: isFinite(rate) AND rate > 0 (rejects 0, NaN, Infinity, negative). False -> LOG the invalid rate and RETURN THE ORIGINAL amount unconverted. True -> convert. This second guard catches a successful fetch that nonetheless yields a garbage rate. [SCOR OE4 | source: RA e08 invalid-rate guard]

nodes.name: Amount needs base-currency value
nodes.documentation: Trigger: any transaction amount (requisition total, quote price, PO line value, invoice amount, payment) needs its base-currency equivalent for thresholds, landed-cost comparison, and analytics. Entry point convertToBase(amount, currency). The base/reporting currency is configurable per tenant (NOT hard-coded INR or OMR). [SCOR OE4 | source: platform-services FX + RA e08]

nodes.name: Base value available (or original, flagged)
nodes.documentation: Either a normalized base amount with full FX context, or (on degradation) the original amount flagged unconverted. Either way the call returned without throwing and the cycle proceeded. [SCOR OE4 | source: platform-services FX]

flows.guard: base or null: return unchanged, no call
0 / NaN / Inf / negative -> log, return original
